summary: The number of students in temporary housing using yellow school buses tracks the size of the citywide shelter population, which grew rapidly starting in spring 2022 amid a surge of migrant and asylum-seeking families arriving in New York City and entering shelters — a surge DOE says it supported through its Project Open Arms school-enrollment effort. Shelter numbers later declined sharply as arrivals slowed to a trickle and the city closed dozens of emergency shelters starting in mid-2024, roughly halving the shelter population by late 2025.

sources.label: NYC Public Schools, Fiscal 2024 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing overall, though shelter numbers have since fallen as arrivals slowed
